title: Add rate limiting to FastAPI endpoints
feature_request: Add rate limiting to the API. We're getting too many requests from some clients and need to throttle them.
phase_1_expected_questions: Which endpoints should be rate-limited — all endpoints globally, or specific routes?
What rate limits should be applied (requests per time window)?
Should rate limiting be per-client (by IP or API key) or global across all clients?
What response should be returned when a client exceeds the rate limit (status code, headers, body)?
What storage backend should track rate limit state — in-memory, Redis, or database?
Should rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset) be included in responses?

phase_1_answers.scope: Apply rate limiting as a configurable middleware that defaults to all endpoints but allows per-route overrides via a decorator.
phase_1_answers.limits: Default to 100 requests per minute per client. Per-route overrides can set custom limits.
phase_1_answers.granularity: Per-client by IP address. Do not require API keys for v1.
phase_1_answers.exceeded_response: Return HTTP 429 Too Many Requests with a JSON body containing 'detail' and 'retry_after' fields.
phase_1_answers.storage_backend: Use an in-memory sliding window counter for v1. Design the interface so Redis can be swapped in later without changing the middleware.
phase_1_answers.headers: Yes, include X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset headers on every response.

phase_2_expected_plan_components: RateLimiter middleware class that hooks into FastAPI's middleware stack
Abstract storage backend interface with an in-memory implementation
Sliding window counter algorithm for tracking request counts
Per-route decorator for overriding default limits
HTTP 429 response model with detail and retry_after fields
Rate limit response headers on all responses
Configuration via environment variables or FastAPI settings
phase_4_runtime_check: Start the FastAPI development server with uvicorn. Send requests to a rate-limited endpoint using curl: first verify normal responses include rate limit headers, then send requests exceeding the limit and verify HTTP 429 responses with correct retry_after values. Confirm the sliding window resets after the time window expires.
